key_insight: A hybrid material point method can accurately reproduce large bending deformations of highly flexible slender structures, including cross-section changes and contact-driven instability.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical simulation tool for designing and predicting the behavior of soft robots, deployable structures, tubes, and tape springs under real bending loads.
method: Built a hybrid-MPM framework combining Lagrangian finite elements with Eulerian finite differences.
Modeled canonical three-point bending tests for elastic tubes and tape springs.
Captured coupled lengthwise bending and cross-sectional deformation, including Brazier-type effects.
evidence_signal: The simulations were validated against desktop experiments and classical theory, with the abstract reporting excellent agreement.
watch_item: The framework targets complex contact and large deformation, so accuracy may depend on careful contact modeling and parameter calibration.
paper_title: Numerical and experimental framework for bending elasticity of highly flexible slender structures
